hard.next_step: Paul will get back in a week about the status of the project. He liked Merlin and it's use cases, they do not have any centralized system at the moment.
hard.ais_why: Next step states verbatim: "He liked Merlin and it's use cases, they do not have any centralized system at the moment" - explicit named-product pull from Paul. Full S2P opp with no centralized system in place - greenfield where Merlin and Agentic AI can lead. No Avoma calls on file, so confidence is Medium.

north_star_verdict.headline: Buyer silent ~24 days since Paul's one-week reply pledge lapsed (~19 Jun); the only touches since are automated could-not-connect dials (last 2 Jul), so a single-threaded Qualified deal is thinning while October stays 108 days out.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 29 Jan 2026, 158 days old. Qualified stage, 108 days to close (23 Oct 2026). CloseDate was pushed 31 May -> 23 Oct on 17 Apr, so the original date already slipped once. Last genuine buyer exchange was the ~12 Jun NextStep (Paul's verbal one-week check-in); the reply was due ~19 Jun and is now ~17 days overdue as of 6 Jul. Every activity since (28 May, 9 Jun, 12 Jun, 2 Jul) is rep-side Outreach/Nooks cadence output - four 'Could Not Connect' dials plus one Horizon event email - not buyer engagement. No stage progression, no submission-date milestones, no demo, no RFI/RFP. An EARLY, single-threaded deal with the sole contact non-responsive to repeated dials is losing momentum.
evidence: NextStep (~12 Jun): Paul liked Merlin and its use cases, Quest has no centralized system today, will reply in one week on project status.
Paul's one-week commitment lapsed ~19 Jun; no reply as of 6 Jul (~17 days overdue).
Since the prior sweep the only new activity is a 2 Jul outbound call logged 'Could Not Connect' - a failed rep dial, not buyer engagement.
Repeated automated cadence dials (Chris_PY_2026, Chris_Nooks, Chris High Intent via Nooks) all logged 'Could Not Connect' - the buyer is not picking up.
Only 2 buyer contact roles (Paul Butts, Kathryn Schneider); no Economic Buyer or Decision Maker identified, and Kathryn has never engaged.
No Avoma calls discovered via opp/account/attendee pulls; discovery depth and competitive landscape remain unconfirmed.
Products scoped (Merlin, Merlin Intake) but no pricing, demo, RFI/RFP, or shortlisting recorded.

108 days from a Qualified stage with no demo would require demo, evaluation, pricing and contracting to compress into an aggressive path; current pace does not support 23 Oct without immediate re-engagement and multi-threading.

sources: Salesforce Q2/Q3 field reads (no metrics fields populated)
metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or financial impact is documented anywhere. The NextStep field (Paul Butts, ~12 Jun) notes Quest 'do not have any centralized system at the moment,' which implies manual, fragmented procurement intake, but Paul never named a cost, cycle time, error rate, headcount burden, or ROI target. The pain is qualitative only. No Avoma call exists to add nuance, and no metrics free-text fields are populated. Without a quantified current-state cost, the business case for Merlin cannot be justified to a budget owner. The move is to run a discovery session with Paul and an operations or finance stakeholder to size the cost of decentralized intake (requisition volume, hours, maverick spend) and model the Merlin Intake savings.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer is identified. Neither contact role carries an Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor designation, and no budget-authority free-text field is populated. Paul Butts (Director, Corporate Procurement Business Transformation) is the only engaged contact; a director-level transformation lead often influences but does not own a $110k budget, so the person who signs is unknown. Nothing in the NextStep or activities reveals who controls pricing. Mapping the budget owner is a ranked move: ask Paul who sponsors the intake/S2P initiative and who approves the spend, then get an introduction before pricing.

decision_criteria.narrative: The formal evaluation criteria are undocumented. No BRD, RFP, use-case list, or scoring rubric has been shared, and no workshop or test-case session has occurred. The only signal is Paul's stated interest in 'Merlin and its use cases,' which suggests AI-driven intake automation is a criterion that favors Zycus, but this is inferred from one line, not confirmed against a requirements set. Because Quest has no centralized system today, the criteria may still be forming. The move is to ask Paul for the initiative's must-have requirements (or offer to co-build a one-page requirement checklist) so Zycus can shape the criteria around Merlin's AI strengths early.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer's decision process is unmapped. There is no call sequence to read (no Avoma calls), only a single-threaded relationship with Paul and a dormant second contact (Kathryn Schneider). No approval chain, evaluation timeline, or committee is documented, and Paul's 'get back in a week' framing implies an internal status check whose owner and cadence are unknown. The CloseDate slip from 31 May to 23 Oct (logged 17 Apr) shows the timeline is soft and internally driven. The move is to establish the process explicitly: who evaluates, who approves, and what the decision milestones are between now and any target date.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are entirely unknown at this early stage. As a US new-business S2P deal there is no incumbent contract expiration acting as a forcing function, and much of US commercial motion invoices directly with no PO, so a missing PO is not a concern here. What is unknown is whether Quest, a large healthcare enterprise, will require a full InfoSec review and DPA (likely, given supplier and spend data), whether they redline the Zycus MSA or accept standard paper, and what their legal lead time is. None of this is on the calendar because the deal has not reached pricing. The move is to surface, in the next real conversation, whether Quest has standard terms and who runs InfoSec, so those gates can be planned rather than discovered late.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is real but unowned and unquantified. Paul Butts stated Quest 'do not have any centralized system at the moment' (~12 Jun NextStep), which for a national diagnostics enterprise implies fragmented, manual procurement intake, poor spend visibility, slow cycle times, and likely maverick spend. This is confirmed qualitative pain that maps directly to Merlin Intake's value proposition, and Paul's endorsement of the use cases shows it resonates. What is missing is the named pain owner (a VP of Procurement, an operations leader, or a cost-reduction mandate) and any quantification. The move is to ask Paul who inside Quest feels the decentralization most acutely and what it is costing, then validate with that owner.

sources: OpportunityContactRole (Paul Butts, Director, Corporate Procurement Business Transformation)
Activities: four 'Could Not Connect' dials through 2 Jul
champion.narrative: Paul Butts (Director, Corporate Procurement Business Transformation) is a developing champion, now at risk. The positive is genuine: his role is explicitly transformation-focused, he is the sole active contact, and he personally endorsed Merlin and its use cases, which is real product pull, not politeness. The problem is that his advocacy has not matured and his engagement has cooled: he committed to a one-week status reply (~12 Jun) that never came, he has not introduced anyone in power, has not shared any internal document, and has not responded to four subsequent cadence dials through 2 Jul. His access to the budget owner is unconfirmed. He is a champion in intent but not yet in action. The move is to re-engage him on a channel he will answer (a personalized email or LinkedIn, not another auto-dial) with a healthcare-specific Merlin proof point, then convert his interest into an introduction and an internal timeline.

competition.narrative: No competitor is named in any source, and no Avoma call exists to reveal a shortlist. Paul's statement that Quest has 'no centralized system at the moment' points to a greenfield, meaning there is no incumbent to displace and Zycus could be positioned as a first mover, but it could equally mean Quest is quietly evaluating several vendors to solve the centralization problem without disclosing the field to us. The genuine current threat is not a named rival but inertia: the do-nothing / internal-delay path, where the project stays deprioritized and the October date slides indefinitely, which is exactly what the 17-day-overdue reply and repeated non-answers suggest. The move is to ask Paul directly who else they are considering and how they handle intake today, then position Merlin's AI differentiation once the competitive set is known.

deal_movement.summary: No forward movement since the prior sweep. The only new activity is a 2 Jul outbound call logged 'Could Not Connect' - the fourth failed cadence dial in a row. The buyer has not re-engaged; last genuine two-way touch remains the ~12 Jun NextStep. The one dated field change on record is the 17 Apr CloseDate push from 31 May to 23 Oct.

competitive_position.summary: No named competitor exists in any source across this or the prior sweep; Competitors__c is blank, no Avoma call was found, and the NextStep names no rival. Quest having 'no centralized system today' (~12 Jun) reads as greenfield: no incumbent to displace, Zycus potentially first mover. The single strongest CURRENT threat is therefore not a vendor but inertia - the do-nothing / internal-delay path, reinforced by a reply that is ~17 days overdue and four unanswered cadence dials through 2 Jul. Until we get Paul back on a live conversation we cannot confirm whether a silent competitive bake-off is underway, so the move is to ask him directly who else they are evaluating and how they handle intake today.

Break the single thread: Kathryn Schneider (Strategic Sourcing Manager) is on the opp but has never engaged - open a parallel line so the deal does not depend solely on a now-silent Paul.
Map the money before pricing: no budget owner is identified, so use the re-engagement to learn who sponsors and approves the intake initiative and secure an introduction.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is the strongest card on this deal and is landing: Paul explicitly liked Merlin and its use cases on a greenfield with no centralized system, so the agentic intake narrative resonates. It is under-leveraged only because the buyer has gone quiet before a demo could deepen it.

No Avoma calls exist to over- or under-rate the field, so the Moderate read stands: real AI appetite, unconfirmed depth.

No quantified business case; a $110k spend cannot be justified to an approver on qualitative 'no centralized system' pain alone.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling, not dead. Paul showed real interest but has gone silent for ~24 days and is not answering cadence dials; the deal is thinning and needs a channel switch to recover.

customer_preference.evidence: Paul liked Merlin and its use cases (~12 Jun), but there is no decisive Zycus-specific next step and he has since gone silent for ~24 days; positive product interest has not converted into a lean or momentum.

business_case.evidence: Only qualitative pain ('no centralized system at the moment'); no ROI, savings, volume, or cycle-time figure articulated by the customer.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A greenfield Qualified deal where our best card (Merlin AI intake, which Paul liked on ~12 Jun) is real but stalling: the sole contact has gone silent for ~24 days, four cadence dials failed, and no budget owner, demo, or business case exists yet - so the win hinges on re-opening a live conversation and opening a second thread before it drifts to no-decision.

win_position.text: We are early and thin: Quest is Qualified with one interested-but-silent contact, no Economic Buyer, no demo, and no competitor intel, so the position is promising on AI fit but unproven on everything else.

Merlin's AI intake story is landing on a greenfield - Paul Butts endorsed 'Merlin and its use cases' on a company with no centralized system (~12 Jun NextStep), which is genuine product pull, not politeness.

The deal is single-threaded on Paul, who has not replied to his own one-week status pledge (~17 days overdue) or to four cadence dials through 2 Jul - if he stays dark there is no second relationship to fall back on.
No budget owner is identified and the pain is unquantified, so even if Paul re-engages there is no path yet to justify a $110k spend to an approver.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum has stalled: the only activity since the prior sweep is a 2 Jul 'Could Not Connect' dial, the fourth failed attempt in a row, with no buyer-initiated touch.
The CloseDate already slipped once (31 May to 23 Oct on 17 Apr) before any demo or evaluation, signaling an internally-driven, soft timeline.
No customer-named dates, no next meeting requested, and no demo booked - the deal has not advanced past initial interest despite being 158 days old.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer owes us the promised project-status update (due ~19 Jun) and has not delivered it, and is not answering outreach - commitment is weak.
Paul did voluntarily express interest in Merlin's use cases, a small positive commitment signal that gives us a reason to re-engage.

deal_risk.text: The dominant threat is inertia / no-decision, not a competitor: Quest keeps running decentralized procurement manually and the project quietly deprioritizes, which the overdue reply and unanswered dials already suggest.
Single-thread plus unmapped economic buyer means one silent director can freeze the entire deal.
